skill.name: figjam-plugin
skill.description: Guides FigJam plugin development, including main-thread code, UI iframe code, WebSocket communication, JSON import validation, builds, and Figma Desktop debugging.
skill.summary: Develop, build, import, and debug FigJam plugins with the expected project workflow.
skill.author: 7nohe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and weak-crypto keywords, but the inspected context shows documentation paths, prose, and one normal local build command. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Local Build Command Requires Normal Tooling Caution
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run `cd packages/plugin && bun run build`. This is a legitimate plugin build step, but it executes local package scripts and should be run only in a trusted checkout.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present in a bash block and executes project tooling. The context is a normal build workflow, so the risk is low rather than suspicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are Documentation
low_findings.description: The static external command findings mostly point to inline paths, file names, and a communication diagram. These entries do not execute shell commands or accept user-controlled command input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown inline code, file paths, or a fenced diagram. They contain no executable Ruby backticks or dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings map to ordinary words such as description text, nodes, edges, and Figma Desktop instructions. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain prose and plugin workflow terms, not MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or related cryptographic calls. No evidence of weak cryptographic implementation was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command in Build Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a shell build command for Bun. This is expected for a development workflow, but it is still an external command pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fenced bash command is explicit and executable. The surrounding build section gives legitimate context and no malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Build FigJam Plugins with Clear Debugging
content.value_statement: FigJam plugin projects can be hard to debug because the main thread and UI iframe behave differently. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused workflow for plugin builds, imports, JSON handling, and WebSocket issues.
actual_capabilities: Explains the FigJam plugin main-thread and UI iframe responsibilities.
Identifies key plugin files such as code.ts, ui.ts, ui.html, and manifest.json.
Guides the local Bun build command for the plugin package.
Describes how to import the development manifest in Figma Desktop.
Helps debug UI, main-thread, and WebSocket connection issues.
Clarifies JSON import validation and IR normalization behavior.
limitations: It does not include source code for the plugin itself.
It assumes the repository already has the expected package layout.
It does not verify external WebSocket servers or runtime credentials.
It does not replace Figma API documentation for unsupported APIs.

use_cases.title: Set Up Plugin Development
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to build the plugin and import the development manifest into Figma Desktop.

use_cases.title: Debug WebSocket Connection Problems
use_cases.description: Trace issues between the CLI, UI iframe, postMessage bridge, and plugin main thread.

use_cases.title: Validate JSON Import Behavior
use_cases.description: Review how DSL JSON and IR JSON should be validated, normalized, and posted to the main thread.

prompt_templates.title: Understand the Plugin Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the FigJam plugin architecture in this repository and list which files handle the main thread, UI iframe, and manifest.

prompt_templates.title: Build and Import the Plugin
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk me through building the FigJam plugin and importing the generated manifest into Figma Desktop for development testing.

prompt_templates.title: Debug WebSocket Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me debug a WebSocket connection issue between the CLI server, plugin UI, and main thread. Show the likely checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Review JSON Import Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the JSON import flow for DSL and IR inputs. Check validation, normalization, and error reporting across UI and main thread boundaries.

output_examples.input: Why does my plugin UI connect but nothing appears on the FigJam canvas?
output_examples.output: A focused checklist covering UI console status, postMessage delivery, main-thread console errors, and canvas rendering logic.

output_examples.input: How do I test a local build in Figma Desktop?
output_examples.output: A short sequence for running the plugin build, finding the generated output, and importing the manifest through the development plugin menu.
A troubleshooting path that separates DSL input, IR input, schema validation, normalization, and UI alert messages.

best_practices: Keep main-thread Figma API logic separate from UI iframe browser logic.
Check both UI DevTools and Figma DevTools when debugging runtime issues.
Validate and normalize JSON before sending data to the plugin main thread.
anti_patterns: Do not call browser-only APIs from the plugin main thread.
Do not bypass JSON validation before rendering imported nodes and edges.
Do not assume WebSocket connection success means canvas rendering succeeded.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps develop and debug a FigJam plugin workflow that uses a main thread, UI iframe, and WebSocket communication.

faq.question: Does it include plugin source code?
faq.answer: No. It documents workflow expectations and key files for an existing plugin project.

faq.question: Which build tool does it reference?
faq.answer: It references Bun for building the plugin package.

faq.question: Can it debug UI and main-thread errors?
faq.answer: Yes. It points to the correct DevTools surfaces for UI iframe errors and main-thread errors.

faq.question: Does it support JSON import troubleshooting?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers DSL JSON, IR JSON, validation, normalization, and visible validation errors.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk from a normal local build command and no evidence of malicious behavior.
